content:
```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["Hot Wallet, Hot Potato! (Don't hold large amounts in an internet-connected wallet, treat it like a hot potato you don't want to hold for too long)."],
  "keyPoints": ["A wallet doesn't store crypto; it stores the private keys that prove ownership of crypto on the blockchain.", "Hot wallets are connected to the internet (convenient for frequent use); Cold wallets are offline (secure for long-term storage).", "Your seed phrase (12-24 words) is a human-readable backup of your private key. Anyone with it has full control of your assets."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Confusing a wallet address (public) with a private key (secret). You can share your address, but must never share your private key.", "Assuming a custodial wallet (like an exchange) means you truly 'own' your crypto. If they hold the keys, they control it.", "Not backing up the seed phrase securely. Storing it digitally (e.g., a screenshot) makes it vulnerable to hackers."
}
```
